title: cirsium jorullense subsp. jorullense
intro: Cirsium jorullense subsp. jorullense, also known as the Mexican thistle or prickly thistle, is a species of flowering plant in the sunflower family. Traditionally used in folk medicine, it is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it a potential treatment for wounds and infections. The plant is said to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation and pain, as well as promoting wound healing and fighting off infection. In herbal preparations, Cirsium jorullense subsp. jorullense can be used to make infusions, decoctions, and salves, although its use and preparation should be carefully considered due to potential allergenic and toxic effects.
